included.reason: Always — owns the MVP cut, scope discipline, and traceability to goals. Central given the explicit scope-creep signal (user wanted 'all of the above').
Always — owns feasibility and stack; here also owns the build-vs-reuse decision on TalentOS (c4), left open by the user on purpose.
Mandatory. Hiring domain + candidate PII in resumes + an EXTERNAL client-facing view (g4) = a real auth/authz boundary and data-handling surface. Never droppable on this Brief.
Promoted in. Two user types (recruiter app u1 + client portal u2) and a real daily recruiter workflow; the hero flow (dump -> shortlist under 5 min, s1) is a UX problem as much as a technical one.

excluded.reason: Droppable for MVP: solo+friend team (c3), 'few weeks' timeline (c2). PM acceptance criteria + Engineer-authored tests carry release confidence at this scale. REVISIT before onboarding a second paying agency or before the client portal handles real client data.
